mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarterly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer income, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in real retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series rise concurrently, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts short-term equity momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions, with direction accuracy dropping below 45% when industrial production growth exceeds 2% quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 320 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening and compress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 320, invalidating the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of observed periods, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding economic activity and higher corporate earnings, which consistently generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters following positive GDP growth, or the rank correlation between GDP growth and 20-day returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces business investment and equity earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when industrial production falls for two straight months, with positive forward returns occurring in over 55% of such instances.
